title: anthemis arvensis subsp. acrochordona
intro: Anthemis arvensis subsp. acrochordona, also known as the field chamomile or wild chamomile, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its calming and soothing properties, with medicinal compounds such as apigenin and luteolin contributing to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and alleviating digestive issues and menstrual cramps. Anthemis arvensis subsp. acrochordona can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote a good night's sleep and overall well-being.
